theory.canonical_statement: A point qualifies as a Trawin Zero Point when three conditions hold simultaneously: (1) the topological winding number around the point is non-zero, (2) the Jacobian determinant of the spacetime transformation tends to zero, and (3) an infinite-order phase transition emerges.
theory.technical_interpretation: A point qualifies as a Trawin Zero Point when three conditions hold simultaneously: (1) the topological winding number around the point is non-zero, (2) the Jacobian determinant of the spacetime transformation tends to zero, and (3) an infinite-order phase transition emerges.
